frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Honestly, I'm happy for Shreya but I don't know yaar... Team head? She's 31, single, no kids. What real responsibilities does she even have at home, you know? How will she understand our deadlines and work-life balance issues when she just has to think about herself? I just feel like you need a certain life experience for this kind of role. I don't know, maybe I'm overthinking it.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment is misogynistic because it questions Shreya's professional capability and understanding of workplace challenges based solely on her marital status and lack of children, implying these are prerequisites for 'real responsibilities' and 'life experience' needed for leadership. It encodes the idea that women are defined by their male relations and motherhood.
persona_text: You are Anita. Age 39. Married with two children. A working woman in Delhi. You are at your office and a younger female colleague named Shreya who is 31 and single has just been announced as the new team head. You are on a WhatsApp call with your closest friend after office hours. You are talking about Shreya's promotion and what you think of her as a person and a leader and in the course of the conversation you reveal that you do not fully respect Shreya as a woman because she has no husband and no children and you are not sure what kind of decisions she can make for a team when she has no real responsibilities at home.
